schemas.name: Virtual Auditor

step.text: Install and configure for e-tendering portal
step.name: Step 5

mainEntity.name: When is Class 3 needed instead of Class 2?

acceptedAnswer.text: For government e-tendering portals (GeM, CPPP, IREPS) and e-procurement. If you bid on government contracts, you need Class 3.

mainEntity.name: What is Class 3 DSC used for?

acceptedAnswer.text: Government e-tendering (GeM, IREPS, CPP Portal), high-security e-procurement, and applications requiring enhanced identity assurance.

mainEntity.name: How is Class 3 different from Class 2?

acceptedAnswer.text: Class 3 requires mandatory in-person verification (not video KYC). Highest assurance level. Cost: Rs 2,000-3,500. Processing: 2-5 days.
GeM (Government e-Marketplace), IREPS (Indian Railways), CPP Portal, various state e-procurement portals, and defence procurement.

mainEntity.name: Can I upgrade Class 2 to Class 3?

acceptedAnswer.text: No direct upgrade. Must purchase separate Class 3 DSC. Both can coexist for same person on different tokens.

mainEntity.name: Is Class 3 needed for company registration?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Class 2 sufficient for MCA, IT, GST. Class 3 only for e-tendering and specific high-security government portals.
